response:
Here is the question:

What is the name of the angle formed by two intersecting lines that is greater than 90 degrees?

Options:
  A. Acute angle
  B. Right angle
  C. Obtuse angle
  D. Straight angle

Hmm, I think an acute angle is less than 90 degrees, so that can't be it. And a right angle is exactly 90 degrees, so that's not it either. I'm not really sure what a straight angle is... is that when two lines are on top of each other or something? But I think I remember something about an obtuse angle being bigger than 90 degrees...

My answer is: C. Obtuse angle. I think this is right because, um, I'm pretty sure I read that somewhere...
